free_text: Deuteron induced reactions in C-12 at bombarding
energies of 5 to 10 MeV
The maximum deuteron beam current used was 0.5 mu-A.
Four silicon surface barrier detectors placed
at a distance of 18 cm from the target were used.
The detection of different particles at several
energies required the use of solid state detectors of
various depletion layer thicknesses.
Self-supporting targets of natural carbon with the
thickness of 20 to 40 mu-g/cm2, which corresponded to
an energy loss of 2 to 5 keV in the specified region
of bombarding energies, were mounted in a scattering
chamber at 45 degr. to the beam direction.
The average mean deviation due to
repetition of measurements. Mean deviations of up to
10% in some cases were due to the peak separation
procedure.
The estimated average mean error in the
absolute cross sections.
SD: Corrections in all Subents. Several
Subents were merged: 002+003; 004+007; 008+009.
Subent 010 was split in two, Subent 015 added.
Fig.2 from Nucl.Phys.,A127(1969)95
SD: The accuracy of data presentation has
been revised. Data from Subent 003 added.

E-LVL=3.85 MeV, ANG=120.degr. in E=6.33-6.51 MeV range.
